title: The Coinage Act, 2011
full_text: The Coinage Act, 2011 सिक्का-निर्माण अधिनियम, 2011 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 201111 Act Number: 11 Enactment Date: 2011-09-01 Act Year: 2011 Short Title: The Coinage Act, 2011 Hindi Title: सिक्का-निर्माण अधिनियम, 2011 Long Title: An Act to consolidate the laws relating to coinage and the Mints, the protection of coinage and to provide for the prohibition of melting or destruction of coins and prohibit the making or the possession thereof for issue and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Finance Department: Department of Economic Affairs Enforcement Date: 28-03-2012 Notification: 28th March, 2012, vide notification No. S.O. 628(E), dated 28th March, 2012, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec.. 3 (ii). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Coinage Act, 2011 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II ESTABLISHMENT OF MINTS CHAPTER III COINAGE CHAPTER IV DIMINISHED, DEFACED AND COUNTERFEIT COINS CHAPTER V OFFENCES AND PENALTIES CHAPTER VI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Power to establish and abolish Mints. Section 4. Denominations, dimensions, designs and composition of coins. Section 5. Standard weight and remedy. Section 6. Coin when a legal tender. Section 7. Decimal system of coinage. Section 8. Power to call in coin. Section 9. Power to certain persons to cut, diminished or defaced coins. Section 10. Power to certain persons to cut counterfeit coins. Section 11. Power of Mint to delegate its functions. Section 12. Prohibition of making or melting or destruction of coins. Section 13. Penalty for contravention of section 12. Section 14. Prohibition and penalty for unlawful making, issue or possession of pieces of metal to be used as money. Section 15. Prohibition and penalty for bringing metal piece for use as coin. Section 16. Offences by companies. Section 17. Forfeiture. Section 18. Probation of Offenders Act, 1958 not to apply to offences under this Act. Section 19. Offences to be cognizable, bailable and non-compoundable. Section 20. Amendment of Act 2 of 1934. Section 21. Offences may be tried summarily. Section 22. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 23. Power to remove difficulties. Section 24. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 25. Rules to be laid before Parliament. Section 26. Saving of making other coins at Mints. Section 27. Repeal and savings. Section 28. Continuance of existing coins.
